title: Handling Large Files Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of streams in handling large files in modern Node.js and Express.js applications.

questions.question: Which of the following Node.js features allows you to process a large file bit by bit (chunk by chunk)?
What's the primary drawback of using 'fs.readFile()' for a 2GB video file?
options: It's more secure than cloud storage.
It will crash your server because it tries to load the entire 2GB file into memory.
It's the only way to store files in a database.

questions.question: Which stream method is the most efficient for connecting a Readable stream to a Writable stream?
What is 'Backpressure' in the context of Node.js streams?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
When an input stream sends data faster than an output stream can process it.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Where should you offload heavy processing task (like video transcoding) for a large file?
